channel.title: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: India Formally Launches Its Bid for a 2028-29 Security Council Seat
item.description: India formally launched its campaign for a nonpermanent seat on the United Nations Security Council for the 2028-2029 term in a room full of ambassadors and senior diplomats at the world body’s headquarters in New York City on July 13, including representatives from all permanent Security Council members: Britain, China, France, Russia and the United ... Read more

item.title: How Farmers Are Learning About Restoring Soils and Scaling Agroecology in Kenya
item.description: At Yumbuni Village in Kenya’s Makueni County, farmers from Vihiga and Kakamega counties have travelled over 560 kilometres to join their colleagues in Kathonzweni Ward and see the progress of experiments being carried out on different homemade organic fertilisers and other farm inputs. “In a special way, we are conducting community-led agroecology research, comparing the […]

item.title: Childhood vaccination rate increases slightly, but millions remain unprotected
item.description: Global childhood immunization programmes continued to recover in 2025, but conflict, poverty and growing vaccine hesitancy are still leaving millions vulnerable to preventable diseases, according to new UN data released on Wednesday.

item.title: Looted gold and gum arabic are bankrolling Sudan's war, UN warns
item.description: Alongside gold, the little-known Sudanese commodity gum arabic – used in soft drinks, food, cosmetics and pharmaceuticals – continues to fuel the war.

item.title: Gulf crisis: Guterres calls for de-escalation, warns against return to war
item.description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres voiced deep concern on Wednesday over the continuing military escalation in the Middle East amid ongoing strikes by the United States and Iran over control of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Security Council LIVE: International Criminal Court briefs on Darfur
item.description: The Security Council meets at 3pm on Wednesday for an update on the International Criminal Court's work in Sudan's Darfur region, referred to the Court back in 2005. Focus has recently turned to atrocities in the ongoing war between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF). The briefing comes as the ICC faces mounting pressure: this week, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio launched a campaign to “dismantle” the Court, which prosecutes individuals for genocide, war crimes, crimes against humanity and the crime of aggression. Follow full live coverage below.

item.title: New guidance offers hope for reducing the global toll of dementia
item.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) has issued updated global guidelines aimed at reducing the risk of cognitive decline and dementia, saying that up to 45 per cent of cases may be preventable or delayed by addressing modifiable risk factors throughout life.

item.title: Colombia: Pockets of insecurity challenge ‘vibrant democracy’, Security Council told
item.description: As Colombia awaits the inauguration of its newly elected president, pockets of insecurity remain a challenge for a nation that has overcome decades of conflict, the head of the UN verification team there told the Security Council on Wednesday.

item.title: World News in Brief: Schools closed in West Bank, AI in healthcare, Indigenous rights
item.description: Access to education continues to worsen in the West Bank, the United Nations reported on Wednesday.

item.title: WHO: Urgent Action Needed for the Future of Cancer Care
item.description: One in five people will be diagnosed with cancer in their lifetime, and when the emotional and physical toll on close family members is factored in, an estimated 92 percent of people globally will be affected by cancer at least once in their lifetime. This staggering statistic is the centerpiece of the World Health Organization […]

item.title: Pride: Once Again a Protest
item.description: On the morning of 28 June, riot police sealed off Taksim Square with iron barriers and enforced bans on all weekend gatherings in Istanbul. Marchers pressed ahead anyway, re-emerging from side streets each time police dispersed them. By the end of the day police had detained at least 50 people, including a journalist. It was […]
